description: SQL correctness and performance depend on schema, data distribution, and plan.
body:
Validate important queries with representative schema and explain output.

Derived from reviewed skill survey outputs, including canonical rules, coverage reports, and trusted skill notes. Paraphrased into domain-pack seed form.

Source rule: this is a generalized, paraphrased seed from local skill survey material; apply only with current repository evidence and more specific pack precedence. Use when SQL work touches schema deltas, query shape, transaction ownership, constraints, locks, and data volume. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include migration dry-runs, rollback checks, EXPLAIN output, constraint tests, and fixture data comparisons. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves data loss, long locks, non-rollbackable writes, silent query-plan regressions, and unsafe backfills, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
